steps.title: Find the central claim
steps.content: The text introduces Roy McLendon's Moonlit St. Lucie as typical of Florida Highwaymen paintings, then presents the main claim: art historians suggest that these paintings helped shape popular perceptions of Florida that continue today.

steps.title: Separate examples from purpose
steps.content: Choice A invents a resurgence in popularity. Choice B wrongly makes McLendon's single painting the main source of broader cultural influence. Choice C invents a contrast between 1950s and 1960s reactions and later reactions. The remaining choice captures the argument about Highwaymen paintings helping create a widespread impression of Florida. The answer is D.
